name: Oddval
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a decorative geometric sans serif; the prominent ink traps and stylistic sets confirm a geometric structure with decorative intent rather than a purely neutral grotesque.
summary: A decorative geometric sans with prominent ink traps, extensive OpenType features and Cyrillic support, available as a variable font across nine weights.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Oddval pairs a geometric sans structure with deliberately prominent ink traps that give the letterforms a distinctive character suited to display and editorial use. The family spans nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with an italic, and ships as a variable font. Cyrillic coverage is handled via dedicated stylistic sets including an oval Cyrillic variant, alongside ligatures, fractions, small figures and case-sensitive punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Oddval as a decorative geometric sans serif with prominent ink traps, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family comprises 19 fonts including a variable font, with two weights available free, and offers a wide range of OpenType features including ligatures, stylistic sets, fractions, small figures and support for Cyrillic.

name: Garet
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif with soft character; the rounded, open forms and stylistic sets confirm a geometric classification with humanist-inflected details.
summary: A soft geometric sans from Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, offered in eleven weights with italic, variable font, and strong OpenType support including Cyrillic.
description: Eleven weights from Hairline to Fat, plus italics and a variable font axis, give the family considerable range for editorial and branding work. The soft geometric character is extended through multiple stylistic sets, including alternative letterforms, an oval Cyrillic option and an alternative double-storey 'g', alongside ligatures, fractions, small figures and case-sensitive punctuation. Two weights, Book and Extra Bold, are available free to download.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Garet as a geometric sans serif with soft character, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family ships with 23 fonts including a variable font, two free weights, and a broad set of OpenType features covering ligatures, stylistic sets, localized Cyrillic forms, fractions and more.

name: Karst
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Karst as a geometric sans serif with a contemporary, edgy character, placing it squarely within the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A geometric sans from Typeforward with a sharp, contemporary edge and broad OpenType feature support including multiple stylistic sets and Cyrillic coverage.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Karst spans nine weights from Hairline to Black with matching italics and a variable font, giving it considerable range for both display and text use. The family includes extensive OpenType features: ligatures, fractions, small figures, ordinals, case-sensitive punctuation and three stylistic sets, alongside localised forms and oval Cyrillic alternates for languages using that script. Two weights are available free, with the remainder available to purchase.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Karst as a geometric sans serif with a contemporary, edgy character. The family ships with 19 fonts including a variable font, and offers a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, plus multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Gogh
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif on the product page, with clean balanced forms and circular constructions typical of the genre.
summary: A geometric sans serif by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, spanning ten weights with italic and variable font support across Latin and Cyrillic scripts.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Gogh is a geometric sans offered in ten weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, plus a variable font. The family carries broad OpenType support including ligatures, fractions, small figures, case-sensitive punctuation and multiple stylistic sets. Cyrillic coverage includes localised forms and stylistic sets for oval letter variants, making it capable across multilingual settings.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Gogh as a geometric sans serif with a clean, balanced voice, offering 22 fonts including a variable font and two free styles. The family includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, along with stylistic sets for additional customisation.

name: Bion
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Bion as a geometric sans serif with sharp, precise details, placing it squarely in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A sharp geometric sans by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, offered in ten weights with condensed and italic cuts, a variable font, and solid Cyrillic support.
description: Bion runs from Hairline to Black across both upright and italic styles, with a condensed subfamily and a variable font covering the full range. OpenType features include multiple stylistic sets, localised Cyrillic forms, case-sensitive punctuation, fractions and small figures. The family covers Latin and Cyrillic scripts, making it a practical choice for multilingual typesetting where a clean geometric voice is required.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Bion as a geometric sans serif with sharp, precise details, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family spans 41 fonts across four subfamilies including Regular, Italic, Condensed and Condensed Italic, and includes a variable font. Four styles are available as free downloads. Bion includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, plus several stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Alphazet
designer: Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif with a deliberate slight imbalance, placing it within the geometric category while acknowledging an intentional departure from pure geometric rigidity.
summary: A geometric sans serif with intentional slight imbalance, available as a variable font across nine weights with Cyrillic support.
description: Designed by Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Alphazet pairs a geometric structure with a deliberate touch of imbalance to avoid the coldness of strict geometric sans designs. The family spans nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, and is available as a variable font. Cyrillic support is included alongside an extensive OpenType feature set covering ligatures, stylistic sets, fractions, small figures and case-sensitive punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Alphazet as a geometric sans serif with slight imbalance, offering 19 fonts including a variable font, with two weights available for free. The family includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, plus multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Em One
designer: Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif; the even stroke widths, circular forms and clean construction evident in the specimen confirm this classification.
summary: A geometric sans serif designed for comfortable reading, with full variable font support, Cyrillic coverage and a broad range of OpenType features.
description: Designed by Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Em One comes in nine weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, plus a variable font. The family covers Latin and Cyrillic scripts, with localised forms and two stylistic sets for alternate Cyrillic letterforms. OpenType features include ligatures, fractions, small figures, ordinals, case-sensitive punctuation and a slashed zero.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Em One as a geometric sans serif tuned for comfortable reading, with 19 fonts including two free weights and a variable font. The family includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, alongside multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Steam
classification_notes: Explicitly categorised as Display by the foundry; the layered family structure with shadow, line and diamond variants, plus expressive OpenType stylistic sets, places it firmly in decorative display territory.
summary: A layered display family by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, built across four structural variants each paired with a shadow counterpart.
description: Eight fonts span four structural layers — Base, Shade, Line and Diamonds — each paired with a shadow variant, allowing stacked compositions through careful colour separation. Latin and Cyrillic are both supported, with several Cyrillic-specific stylistic sets including oval forms and alternative letterforms. OpenType features cover ligatures, fractions, small figures and case-sensitive punctuation, giving the family more typographic rigour than most decorative display releases.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Steam as a display typeface with a playful, expressive character, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family ships with 13 fonts across Base, Shade, Line and Diamonds subfamilies, each with shadow variants, and includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions and multiple stylistic sets. One font is available free to download.

name: Oddval Text
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif, tuned for text setting. Shares character with the Oddval display family but optimised for body copy use.
summary: A text-optimised geometric sans with Cyrillic support, variable font axis, and a considered set of OpenType features drawn by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova.
description: Shares its geometric character with the Oddval display family but is specifically tuned for body copy and smaller sizes. The family runs nine weights from Hairline to Black with matching italics, available as individual static fonts or as a variable font. Cyrillic support is provided across standard, oval and alternative oval stylistic sets, with additional OpenType features covering ligatures, fractions, small figures, ordinals and case-sensitive punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Typeforward describe Oddval Text as a geometric sans serif with Oddval's character, tuned for text. The family ships with 19 fonts including a variable font, two of which are free, and offers a wide range of OpenType features including ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, alongside multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.
